codeReview.summary: The implementation replaces stale threadId closure usage with a ref-backed value in useCodex, adds defensive error surfacing for missing client state, and adds targeted message-flow tests covering sequential turns and send-failure recovery. Assigned expected behavior is satisfied.

issues.description: The handleSubmit catch safety-net checks `if (chat.isThinking)` using a closure value captured before `chat.sendMessage` updates state, so this fallback may not run when a rejection occurs immediately. Consider removing the guard or reading latest thinking state from a ref.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `tui-worker` skill requires TDD order ('Write tests FIRST'), but the transcript shows code edits before new tests were created while `skillFeedback.followedProcedure` remained true. This suggests procedure adherence criteria are unclear.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript skeleton for workerSessionId 70ad0371-76e5-407d-9467-702c9e2332b6 shows Edit calls for useCodex.ts and App.tsx before Create of packages/orchestrator-tui/src/__tests__/message-flow.test.tsx.

summary: Reviewed feature tui-message-flow-fix by inspecting feature metadata, worker handoff/transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and commit 63f6172b1c37e9cf19a256114b292477ba34424c diff. The fix satisfies the message-flow contract and test coverage expectations; one non-blocking robustness caveat was noted in App.tsx catch fallback logic.
